questions.question: What is the advantage A(s,a), and why does it fix REINFORCE's variance?
options: A faster optimizer for the critic

A = G − V̂(s) — an action is good if it produced ABOVE-AVERAGE return; subtracting the learned baseline leaves the gradient expectation unchanged but kills most of the noise from raw Monte Carlo returns that swing 10x between episodes
The agent's win rate over a baseline bot
A bonus for unexplored actions
questions.explanation: REINFORCE-with-learned-critic IS actor-critic: the critic gives the actor a low-variance teacher. Every deep-policy method after 2015 (A2C, A3C, PPO, SAC, IMPALA) builds on this.

questions.question: What are the two networks in actor-critic and their jobs?
options: Generator and discriminator

The actor π_θ(a|s) is the policy, sampled to act, trained by policy gradient; the critic V_φ(s) estimates expected return, trained by regression to a value target — the critic's output feeds the actor's advantage
Encoder and decoder
Two identical actors voting
questions.explanation: Both train from the same rollouts under one combined loss: policy gradient + value regression (c_v ≈ 0.5) + entropy bonus (c_e ≈ 0.01) to keep exploration alive.

questions.question: How does GAE generalize the bias-variance trade in advantage estimation?
options: It eliminates both bias and variance
A_GAE = Σ (γλ)^l δ_{t+l} — an exponentially weighted average over all n-step advantages: λ=0 is pure TD (low variance, high bias), λ=1 is MC (unbiased, high variance); λ=0.95 is the 2026 default
It replaces advantages with raw rewards
It samples advantages from a buffer
questions.explanation: Schulman et al. (2016). The same TD-to-MC dial as n-step returns, packaged as one tunable λ — the advantage estimator PPO ships with.

questions.question: How do A2C and A3C differ, and which dominates today?
options: A3C is A2C with attention

A3C runs asynchronous CPU workers applying gradients to a shared parameter server (decorrelation via different trajectories, no replay buffer); A2C synchronizes parallel envs into one batch update — GPU-based A2C dominates in 2026 because GPUs want large batches
A2C is off-policy, A3C on-policy
They differ only in author lists
questions.explanation: A3C (Mnih 2016) proved CPU-scale RL training was possible; hardware then made its asynchrony obsolete — synchronous batching matches the accelerator.

questions.question: Why does the combined loss include an entropy bonus?
options: To regularize the critic's weights
Maximizing H(π_θ(·|s)) discourages premature collapse to a deterministic policy, keeping exploration alive while the advantage signal is still noisy — canonical coefficient c_e ≈ 0.01
To make training deterministic
Entropy measures the replay buffer size
questions.explanation: Without it, the policy can lock onto an early local optimum and stop sampling alternatives — the policy-gradient analogue of ε-greedy's exploration guarantee.
